scoringRubric.0: Absent — not mentioned anywhere in the response
scoringRubric.1: Cited — URL appears in sources/citations but company not named in text
scoringRubric.2: Mentioned — named in a list or discussed as one option among several
scoringRubric.3: Recommended — named as a top choice, leader, or specifically highlighted

categories.name: Solution Discovery
categories.description: Generic buyer-intent queries — would this company show up when someone searches for what they do?

queries.template: What are the best {industry} agencies for mid-market companies?

categories.name: Company-Specific
categories.description: Direct brand queries — what does AI say about this company specifically?

categories.name: Problem-Aware
categories.description: Buyers who know the problem but not the solution — would this company come up?

queries.template: Do I need a {industry} agency or can I do it in-house?
What should I look for in a {industry} provider?
Is {industry} worth the investment for a growing company?

categories.name: Video & Content
categories.description: Content and video discovery queries — does their content appear in AI answers?

queries.template: Who is creating good content about {industry}?

videoScoring.description: YouTube/video presence score (0-10), assessed per company

rubric.1-2: Channel exists but dormant (fewer than 5 videos/year, no recent uploads)
rubric.3-4: Active but low traction (regular uploads, minimal views/engagement)
rubric.5-6: Regular uploads, moderate views, some variety in content types
rubric.7-8: Consistent publishing, good engagement, multiple content formats
rubric.9-10: Strong channel — high engagement, frequent uploads, diverse formats, growing subscriber base

dataToCollect: Upload frequency (videos per month in last 90 days)
Content types (Demo, Thought Leadership, Tutorial, Customer Story, Podcast, Short)
